rationale: The citing paper gives a specific summary of Gal and McShane (2012), stating that debt-snowball users are more likely to eliminate debt after controls, and uses this as direct evidence that the focal phenomenon is real. This citation helps motivate the paper's core research problem: explaining when and why small-victory ordering improves completion behavior. Even though the cited study is not methodologically reused, it plays a meaningful framing role rather than a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: Brown and Lahey (2015) treat Gal and McShane (2012) as prior empirical support that small-victory debt sequencing can improve debt elimination, then build a formal model and lab tests to explain the mechanism more generally. The cited paper functions as an empirical foundation for the motivation and positioning of the new study.
